notes: Run: Priority 1b cleanup, Priority 4 EXP-065, Priority 3 curmudgeon major holes. 5 self-apply patches: ISS-705 notation clarify, SEC-6.1 WIN-012→053 misattribution, WIN-064 duplicate removal, Part 5 Dome Says column fix, Part 5 NMP math clarification.

patches.find: Scoreboard split on the same observation (W004 / WIN-025).
patches.replace: Scoreboard split on the same observation (results.json entry W004, the dome's internal label for the 2024 Eclipse 9-Station Replication — our WIN-025).
patches.rationale: EXP-065 verified W004 in results.json IS the eclipse test (WIN-025). Notation clarification to avoid namespace confusion: dome uses different W-numbering in results.json vs wins page. Severity downgraded from CRITICAL to minor by analyst verification.

patches.find: WIN-012 (Geomagnetic field): The formula is B(r) = 62,376 × e−r/8619 + 64,852 × e−r/8619.
patches.replace: WIN-053 (Geomagnetic field): The formula is B(r) = 62,376 × e−r/8619 + 64,852 × e−r/8619.
patches.rationale: SEC-6.1 misattributes the two-pole B(r) formula to WIN-012 (which is about mag-gravity coupling constant κ=1.67). The formula belongs to WIN-053 (Two-pole geomagnetic model). Curmudgeon alert 2026-04-10T23:55. A dome defender can immediately disprove the WIN attribution.

patches.find: assume spherical wave propagation through concentric spherical layers. On a flat disc 20,015 km in radius, there is no spherical liquid core to refract P-waves — a flat disc would produce simple geometric attenuation with distance, not a sharp angular shadow zone with PKP re-emergence beyond 140°. The dome offers no flat-disc mechanism that predicts these specific cutoff angles. The IRIS Global Seismographic Network (143 stations) and GEOSCOPE (30 stations) have mapped this shadow zone consistently for decades; P-wave arrival times, amplitudes, and slownesses all fit a spherical Earth model to better than 1% precision across thousands of earthquake-station pairs. Notably, the dome's own source files contain no discussion of P-wave shadow zones at any of its seven pages — WIN-064 was added in V51.0 without any explanation of how a flat disc produces this observation.
patches.replace: assume spherical wave propagation through concentric spherical layers.
patches.rationale: WIN-064 detail_evidence has ~700 chars of verbatim duplicated text introduced during the text expansion. The block 'On a flat disc 20,015 km in radius, there is no spherical liquid core...' through '...how a flat disc produces this observation.' appears twice in succession. Second occurrence removed. Curmudgeon alert 2026-04-08T23:30 and WIN-064.c2.json.

patches.find: 6. NMP Drift Rate Failing
patches.replace: 6. NMP Drift Rate PASS (39.9%)
patches.rationale: Verdict table 'Dome Says' column for Test 6 (NMP Drift Rate) showed 'Failing' — but the dome's own dashboard says 'PASS (39.9% error)'. We must report what the dome claims, not our editorial judgment, in the 'Dome Says' column. The 'Our Verdict' column remains 'Refuted by Data'. Curmudgeon alert 2026-04-10T22:30 — 'putting our editorial judgment in a column labeled with the dome's voice is exactly the mischaracterization we criticize the dome for.'

patches.find: ~22.8 km/yr for 2026. On the dome's own terms, this NMP prediction requires ad hoc tolerance widening to avoid failing.
patches.replace: ~22.8 km/yr for 2026 — a 34.9% deviation by our own calculation (the dome dashboard reports 39.9%, reflecting a different comparison baseline; see Key Issue note). On the dome's own terms, this NMP prediction requires ad hoc tolerance widening to avoid failing.
patches.rationale: NMP error math discrepancy: our stated values (22.8 vs ~35 km/yr) give |22.8-35|/35 = 34.9%, not 39.9%. We cited the dome's self-reported figure without noting the discrepancy. A dome defender checking our arithmetic would catch this. Curmudgeon alert 2026-04-10T22:30.
